---
title: "Analysis of Factors Affecting Well-Being"
author: "Vladyslav Rudenko"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.08
execute-dir: project
---

## Project idea

I analyzed which factors are related to well-being, stress and productivity.

The main focus was on:

- sleep
- stress
- physical activity
- daily habits
- productivity and work-life balance

---

## Datasets

| Dataset | Target |
|---|---|
| Lifestyle | wellness |
| Productivity | productivity |
| Sleep | stress_level |
| Students | stress_numeric |
| Remote work | productivity_score |

I analyzed the datasets separately because they describe different groups of people.

---

## Methods

I used two main methods:

**Correlation analysis**  
Shows which variables are directly related to the target.

**PCA**  
Shows the main structure of the dataset and reduces many variables into several principal components.

---

## Lifestyle dataset

Target: **wellness**

![](../data/plots/pca___wellness_correlation.png){width=82%}

This plot shows which lifestyle factors are most related to wellness.

---

## Productivity dataset

Target: **productivity**

![](../data/plots/productivity_correlation.png){width=82%}

This helped me see which daily habits are connected with higher or lower productivity.

---

## Sleep dataset

Target: **stress_level**

![](../data/plots/sleep___stress_correlation.png){width=82%}

This dataset was useful because stress is strongly connected with sleep and health variables.

---

## Student dataset

Target: **stress_numeric**

![](../data/plots/students___stress_correlation.png){width=82%}

The goal was to see how student lifestyle factors are related to stress.

---

## Remote work dataset

Target: **productivity_score**

![](../data/plots/remote___productivity_correlation.png){width=82%}

This part focused on productivity in remote work conditions.

---

## Conclusion

The same types of variables appeared repeatedly:

- sleep
- stress
- physical activity
- daily habits
- lifestyle or work-life balance

The analysis does not prove causality, but it shows clear statistical relationships in the available data.
